description: Antiship combat behavior: missile (munition) properties and per-launcher-type loadouts. Names sourced from the TaiwanDefenseRefactor sister repo. This file owns COMBAT BEHAVIOR only; container layout lives in the grouping spec and display names in antiship_systems_consolidated.json.

20._comment: Cheng Kung Frigates - 8x HF each (HF-III, shared HF store)

21._comment: Kang Ding Frigates - 8x HF each (HF-II, shared HF store)

16._comment: Kwang Hua VI Patrol Boats - 4x HF-II each

17._comment: Tuo Chiang Corvettes - 12 missiles each (HF-III, shared HF store)

23._comment: Mobile Coastal AShM Launchers (HF) - 26 launchers, 4x HF-II each, shared HF store

24._comment: Mobile Coastal AShM Launchers (Harpoon) - 100 launchers, 4x Harpoon_Ground_II each

HF._comment: Interchangeable Hsiung Feng II/III inventory (was 340 + 320). Munitions tagged store_group=HF draw their inventory from this shared budget; their individual quantity fields are retained for documentation only and ignored by the engine.

notes: munitions: keyed by missile name. quantity is the theater-wide global pool. in_flight_failure_rate is per-missile loss probability before reaching the fleet.

store_group: when a munition declares one, its inventory is drawn from store_groups[group].quantity (a shared budget) instead of its own quantity. Hsiung Feng II and III share the 'HF' store because their physical magazines are interchangeable.
launchers: keyed by antiship type_id (string). missiles is the loadout list in priority order - a launcher fires the first munition with remaining global pool, falling back to the next.
range_tier: own_to (fires only into its own TO), neighboring (own + TO_ADJACENCY neighbors), whole_island (any TO).

2026 OOB breakout: type 1 split into 19-22 (Keelung/Cheng Kung/Kang Ding/Chi Yang); type 4 split into 23 (HF) + 24 (Harpoon). HF-firing classes each declare a single primary HF variant; the II/III mix emerges at fleet level and all draw the shared HF store.
Phase 2 will add type_id 7 (UAV) and 8 (USV) as self-attacking single-use entries.
Seed values are conservative; Phase 3 tunes against the sister repo.
